description: S3 protocol compatibility — close gaps in ACL/policy/IAM/checksums/SSE/notifications/website/replication/auth so Strata passes >=80% of Ceph s3-tests executable subset.

userStories.title: Parse AccessControlPolicy XML body for PutBucketAcl/PutObjectAcl
userStories.description: As a client, I want PutBucketAcl/PutObjectAcl to accept a full XML grant list, not just the canned x-amz-acl header, so I can grant fine-grained permissions to specific principals.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse <AccessControlPolicy> with <Owner> and <AccessControlList><Grant> entries
Persist grant list in meta.Store via new SetBucketGrants/GetBucketGrants and SetObjectGrants/GetObjectGrants methods (memory + cassandra)
GetBucketAcl/GetObjectAcl return the persisted grants when present, fall back to canned otherwise
Reject grants with unknown grantee Type or Permission values with 400 MalformedACLError
Unit tests cover: round-trip XML body, mixed canned header + body, malformed body 400, unknown grantee type 400
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes
userStories.notes: Grants stored as JSON blob (bucket_acl_grants table for buckets, grants column on objects). Memory store uses bucketGrants/objectGrants maps.

userStories.title: Add IAM-style policy evaluator package internal/auth/policy
userStories.description: As a follow-on for bucket policy enforcement, I need a standalone evaluator that takes a policy document, principal, action, resource, and context, and returns Allow/Deny.
acceptanceCriteria: New package internal/auth/policy with Document, Statement, Evaluate(doc, principal, action, resource, ctx) (Decision, error)
Supports Effect (Allow/Deny), Principal (string, list, or *), Action (string or list, with s3:* wildcards), Resource (string or list, with arn wildcards), Condition (StringEquals only)
Explicit Deny overrides Allow
Default decision when no statement matches: Deny
Unit tests cover: explicit allow, explicit deny override, wildcard action, wildcard resource, StringEquals condition match/miss, anonymous principal *
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... passes
userStories.notes: Package internal/auth/policy: Decision (Allow/Deny), Document, Statement, Principal (string '*' or {AWS:[]}), StringOrSlice for Action/Resource, glob matcher for s3:* and ARN wildcards, StringEquals condition only, explicit Deny overrides. Default Deny on no match or nil doc. Parse() validates Effect.

userStories.title: Wire policy evaluator into request middleware and object handlers
userStories.description: As a bucket owner, I want my JSON bucket policy to actually gate object-level requests; today it is stored but never enforced.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/auth middleware loads the bucket's policy document via meta.Store after SigV4/anonymous identity is resolved
Object handlers (PutObject, GetObject, HeadObject, DeleteObject, multipart endpoints) call requireAccess(action, arn) before any state change
Anonymous identity passes through evaluator with principal '*'
Policy denial returns 403 AccessDenied
Unit tests: anonymous GET allowed by s3:GetObject Allow, anonymous GET denied without, explicit Deny overrides Allow, wrong-principal Allow denied
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/s3api/access.go added requireAccess(b, action, arn). Loads bucket policy via Meta.GetBucketPolicy (ErrNoSuchBucketPolicy → bypass), parses with policy.Parse, evaluates with principalForRequest (anonymous → '*'). 403 AccessDenied on Deny/no-match. Wired into handleObject for PUT/GET/HEAD/DELETE + initiateMultipart, uploadPart, completeMultipart, abortMultipart, listParts, copyObject. ?acl/?tagging/?retention/?legal-hold subresources are out of scope.

userStories.title: Enforce bucket and object ACL grants on object-level requests
userStories.description: As a bucket owner, when bucket ACL is public-read, anonymous GETs should succeed; when private, they should 403. Today the canned ACL is stored but never consulted on the request path.
acceptanceCriteria: Anonymous GET /<bucket>/<key> succeeds when bucket ACL is public-read or public-read-write
Anonymous PUT succeeds only when ACL is public-read-write
Per-object grants from US-001 override bucket ACL on per-object reads
ACL check runs after policy check; explicit policy Deny still wins
Unit tests cover four canned values × {GET, PUT, DELETE} × {anonymous, owner, alt principal}
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/s3api/access.go grew requireACL + cannedAllows + grantsAllow + granteeMatches helpers; requireObjectAccess wraps requireAccess (policy) then requireACL (canned + persisted grants). Owner FC: info.Owner==b.Owner short-circuits (anonymous-owned bucket + anonymous request still passes since both Owner=='anonymous'). Per-object grants override bucket ACL on s3:GetObject only. testutil_test.go gained X-Test-Principal injection so tests can simulate owner/alt principals.

userStories.title: Add Cassandra-backed credentials store
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to add and rotate access keys without restarting the gateway, so I can onboard new clients in production.
acceptanceCriteria: New cassandra.CredentialsStore implementing the auth.Store interface against the access_keys table
Schema migration adds access_keys (access_key text PK, secret_key text, owner text, disabled boolean, created_at timestamp) via additive ALTER
Composable auth.MultiStore tries static credentials first, then Cassandra
60s in-memory cache with refresh-on-miss and explicit Invalidate(accessKey)
Unit tests cover lookup hit/miss/disabled, key rotation, deleted-key returns auth.ErrCredentialNotFound
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... passes
userStories.notes: auth.CredentialsStore is the existing iface (not auth.Store); auth.ErrNoSuchCredential is the existing sentinel (not auth.ErrCredentialNotFound) — used both verbatim. New cassandra.CredentialStore (internal/meta/cassandra/credentials.go) selects from access_keys (PK=access_key); disabled rows are surfaced as ErrNoSuchCredential. Schema: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS access_keys appended to tableDDL (additive). New auth.MultiStore (internal/auth/multi.go) tries stores in order, caches positive AND negative results for ttl, exposes Invalidate(accessKey); transient (non-ErrNoSuchCredential) errors propagate without caching. Wired in cmd/strata-gateway/main.go: static store first; cassandra.CredentialStore appended only when meta backend is cassandra (via metaStore.(*metacassandra.Store).Session()). DefaultCacheTTL = 60s exported for callers.

userStories.title: Add IAM users + access keys storage and CreateUser/ListUsers/DeleteUser endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin tool, I want to call POST /?Action=CreateUser etc. against the gateway to provision principals so s3-tests fixtures ([iam root], [iam alt root]) can spin up isolated identities.
acceptanceCriteria: iam_users table in cassandra (user_name PK, user_id, path, created_at) plus mirror in memory store
internal/s3api/iam.go routes ?Action=CreateUser, ?Action=GetUser, ?Action=ListUsers, ?Action=DeleteUser
AWS-style XML form-encoded request/response shapes match the iam:CreateUser docs (CreateUserResponse + CreateUserResult > User)
Hardcoded [iam root] credentials gate all ?Action= endpoints
Unit tests cover round-trip CreateUser → GetUser → ListUsers → DeleteUser, double-create returns 409 EntityAlreadyExists, delete-missing returns 404 NoSuchEntity
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.IAMUser + ErrIAMUserNotFound/ErrIAMUserAlreadyExists added to internal/meta/store.go; CreateIAMUser/GetIAMUser/ListIAMUsers/DeleteIAMUser are part of meta.Store. Memory backend mirrors via iamUsers map[string]*meta.IAMUser. Cassandra: new iam_users table (user_name PK, user_id, path, created_at) with LWT IF NOT EXISTS / IF EXISTS to map ErrIAMUserAlreadyExists / ErrIAMUserNotFound. New internal/s3api/iam.go exports IAMRootPrincipal="iam-root" — handleIAM gates every ?Action= endpoint on info.Owner==IAMRootPrincipal (anonymous fails). Action dispatch reads Action from query OR application/x-www-form-urlencoded body via extractIAMAction. Wired in ServeHTTP: bucket=="" + Action!="" → handleIAM. Response shapes match AWS IAM XML (CreateUserResponse > CreateUserResult > User; ListUsersResponse > ListUsersResult > Users > member); errors are EntityAlreadyExists (409), NoSuchEntity (404), ValidationError (400), InvalidAction (400).

userStories.title: Add CreateAccessKey/ListAccessKeys/DeleteAccessKey IAM endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin tool, I want CreateAccessKey to mint a (key, secret) pair tied to a user so s3-tests fixtures can authenticate.
acceptanceCriteria: ?Action=CreateAccessKey, ?Action=ListAccessKeys, ?Action=DeleteAccessKey routed in internal/s3api/iam.go
CreateAccessKey writes to access_keys (US-005 schema) with user_name foreign key and returns AccessKey + SecretAccessKey in XML once
DeleteAccessKey removes the row and invalidates the cache (US-005 Invalidate)
Hardcoded [iam root] gate enforced
Unit tests cover CreateAccessKey → use → DeleteAccessKey → 403 InvalidAccessKeyId on next request
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.IAMAccessKey + ErrIAMAccessKeyNotFound added to internal/meta/store.go; meta.Store grew CreateIAMAccessKey/GetIAMAccessKey/ListIAMAccessKeys/DeleteIAMAccessKey. Memory backend mirrors via accessKeys map (PK=AccessKeyID); new memory.CredentialStore (internal/meta/memory/credentials.go) implements auth.CredentialsStore over the same map and is wired in cmd/strata-gateway/main.go for memory-meta gateways. Cassandra: ALTER TABLE access_keys ADD user_name (additive); new iam_access_keys_by_user lookup table (PK (user_name, access_key)); new internal/meta/cassandra/access_keys.go uses logged BATCH for create/delete to keep both tables consistent; cassandra.CredentialStore.Lookup unchanged (still SELECTs from access_keys). cassandra.CredentialStore.Put/Delete removed — IAM writes go through meta.Store. iam.go switch grew CreateAccessKey/ListAccessKeys/DeleteAccessKey; CreateAccessKey returns SecretAccessKey ONCE in CreateAccessKeyResponse > CreateAccessKeyResult > AccessKey (Status=Active|Inactive, AKIA-prefixed, 30-byte base64 secret). ListAccessKeys returns AccessKeyMetadata members (no secret). DeleteAccessKey calls Server.InvalidateCredential(accessKey) when set; main.go assigns multi.Invalidate. Errors: ValidationError 400 (missing UserName/AccessKeyId), NoSuchEntity 404 (missing user, missing access key).

userStories.title: Implement minimal STS AssumeRole endpoint
userStories.description: As a multi-account test fixture, I want temporary credentials with an expiry so cross-account scenarios in s3-tests can run.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /?Action=AssumeRole returns AccessKeyId, SecretAccessKey, SessionToken, Expiration (1h default)
SigV4 middleware honors x-amz-security-token header against the temporary key store
Token storage in-memory with TTL eviction (acceptable given short expiry)
Expired token → 403 ExpiredToken
Unit tests cover assume → use → expire → 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: auth.STSStore (internal/auth/sts.go) implements CredentialsStore over an in-memory map keyed by ASIA-prefixed AccessKey. Issue() mints (AccessKey, SecretKey, SessionToken) and stores Expiration; Lookup returns ErrExpiredToken on expired sessions (and evicts). auth.Credential.SessionToken added; middleware compares the request's x-amz-security-token against cred.SessionToken (header- or presigned-query-borne) — mismatch → ErrInvalidToken (403 InvalidToken). MultiStore skips caching creds with SessionToken so token expiry/revoke takes effect immediately. New iamAssumeRole handler in internal/s3api/sts.go gates on IAMRoot like other ?Action= endpoints; requires RoleArn + RoleSessionName, accepts optional DurationSeconds (900..43200, default 3600s). main.go prepends an STSStore to the credential chain and wires apiHandler.STS = sts. SetClock test hook drives expiry deterministically.

userStories.title: Verify and persist x-amz-checksum-* headers on PutObject (CRC32, CRC32C, SHA1, SHA256, CRC64NVME)
userStories.description: As an AWS SDK v3 client (which sends checksums by default), I want my PutObject requests with x-amz-checksum-<algo> to succeed and have the checksum stored.
acceptanceCriteria: Add meta.Object.Checksums map[string]string with additive Cassandra schema migration
Parse x-amz-checksum-<algo> request headers; verify against streamed body content during PutChunks
Mismatch → 400 BadDigest
Return x-amz-checksum-<algo> on GetObject and HeadObject
Five algorithms supported: crc32, crc32c, sha1, sha256, crc64nvme (vendor a small CRC64NVME implementation if needed; AWS test vector covered)
Unit tests per algorithm: positive, mismatch, missing optional
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Object.Checksums map[string]string added (additive ALTER objects ADD checksums map<text,text>; cassandra PutObject INSERT + GetObject SELECT extended). internal/s3api/checksum.go centralises algos: CRC32 (IEEE), CRC32C (Castagnoli), SHA1, SHA256, CRC64NVME (vendored — table-based reflected impl, polynomial 0x9a6c9329ac4bc9b5, init/xor 0xFFFF...; verified against AWS test vector 0xae8b14860a799888 for '123456789'). parseRequestChecksums extracts every x-amz-checksum-<algo> header for a known algo (unknown headers are simply ignored — silent-OK is fine because AWS SDKs only set known ones); malformed base64 → 400 InvalidArgument. checksumWriter fans body bytes to all hashers via io.TeeReader on the put path; verifyChecksums compares after PutChunks and on mismatch deletes the just-written manifest and returns 400 BadDigest. writeChecksumHeaders echoes all persisted x-amz-checksum-<algo> on GetObject + HeadObject. Tests round-trip per-algo, mismatch-per-algo (404 follow-up confirms no object persisted), missing-headers no-op, and invalid base64 rejection.

userStories.title: Multipart UploadPart and CompleteMultipartUpload checksum support
userStories.description: As an SDK v3 client uploading multipart, I want each part's checksum verified and the composite checksum returned by CompleteMultipartUpload.
acceptanceCriteria: UploadPart accepts and verifies x-amz-checksum-<algo> per part
Per-part checksum stored in meta.MultipartPart.Checksums
CompleteMultipartUpload computes composite checksum (algo-specific concat-and-hash rule) and returns x-amz-checksum-<algo> header
Mismatch on any part → 400 BadDigest from UploadPart, never reaches Complete
Unit tests round-trip a 3-part multipart with crc32 and verify response
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.MultipartPart.Checksums map[string]string round-trips through both backends (cassandra schema appended additive ALTER multipart_parts ADD checksums map<text,text>; SavePart INSERT and ListParts SELECT extended). uploadPart in internal/s3api/multipart.go now wraps the body with the same parseRequestChecksums + io.TeeReader(checksumWriter) + verifyChecksums chain used by putObject; mismatch deletes the just-written manifest and returns 400 BadDigest. On success the part's Checksums map is persisted via SavePart and echoed via writeChecksumHeaders on the 200. completeMultipart runs ListParts before CompleteMultipartUpload to grab per-part Checksums by part number, then composeMultipartChecksums (in checksum.go) takes algos present on every requested part, base64-decodes each part's digest, runs the algo over the concatenation, and returns base64(hash(concat))-N. The composite is set on obj.Checksums (so GetObject/HeadObject echo it) AND emitted on the response as both x-amz-checksum-<algo> headers and the corresponding ChecksumCRC32/ChecksumCRC32C/ChecksumSHA1/ChecksumSHA256/ChecksumCRC64NVME XML elements (omitempty). Tests in multipart_checksum_test.go: 3-part CRC32 happy path verifies the part-echo header, the composite header, the XML composite field, and the GET-side echo; per-part mismatch returns 400 BadDigest and ListParts shows no persisted part; complete with no part checksums emits no Checksum* fields (composite is nil).

userStories.title: SSE-S3 metadata storage (passthrough) on objects and bucket default
userStories.description: As a client setting x-amz-server-side-encryption: AES256, I want the header accepted, persisted, and echoed back on Get/Head — even though Strata does not actually encrypt at rest yet.
acceptanceCriteria: meta.Object.SSE field stores algorithm string (additive Cassandra ALTER)
PutObject + InitiateMultipartUpload accept x-amz-server-side-encryption (AES256 only for now; reject aws:kms with NotImplemented)
GetObject/HeadObject return the same header
PutBucketEncryption/GetBucketEncryption/DeleteBucketEncryption endpoints round-trip the XML config blob via the existing setBucketBlob helpers
When bucket encryption default is set and a PutObject omits the header, the default is applied
Unit tests: round-trip per object, round-trip per bucket default, default applied on PUT
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Object.SSE + meta.MultipartUpload.SSE round-trip through both backends (cassandra: additive ALTER objects ADD sse text + ALTER multipart_uploads ADD sse text; PutObject INSERT, GetObject SELECT, CreateMultipartUpload INSERT, GetMultipartUpload SELECT extended). Memory backend round-trips via shallow-copy. New meta.ErrNoSuchEncryption sentinel + Set/Get/DeleteBucketEncryption interface methods, both backends use the existing setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob/deleteBucketBlob blob helpers. internal/s3api/encryption.go: new ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration XML, putBucketEncryption/getBucketEncryption/deleteBucketEncryption, resolveSSE(r,b) — request header wins (validated), bucket default applies as fallback, missing config → no SSE. validateSSEAlgorithm: AES256 OK; aws:kms / aws:kms:dsse → 501 NotImplemented; everything else → 400 InvalidArgument. internal/s3api/server.go: ?encryption route added to handleBucket; putObject calls resolveSSE before PutChunks (so a kms/bogus header rejects the request before any data lands), persists obj.SSE; getObject sets x-amz-server-side-encryption header. internal/s3api/multipart.go: initiateMultipart resolves SSE, persists on mu.SSE, echoes header on InitiateMultipartUploadResult; uploadPart echoes mu.SSE on the part response; completeMultipart copies mu.SSE to obj.SSE and emits the header. New error codes ErrNoSuchEncryption (404 ServerSideEncryptionConfigurationNotFoundError) / ErrInvalidEncryptionAlgorithm (400) / ErrKMSNotImplemented (501). Tests in internal/s3api/encryption_test.go: per-object round-trip (PUT+GET+HEAD echo), aws:kms rejected, unknown algo rejected, bucket config CRUD, default applied on header-less PUT, bucket-level kms put rejected, malformed body rejected, multipart end-to-end with SSE round-trip through Initiate→UploadPart→Complete→HEAD.

userStories.title: SSE-C customer-key headers — accept, validate, gate reads
userStories.description: As a client uploading with SSE-C, I want the gateway to accept and validate the customer key headers and reject Get requests that omit them.
acceptanceCriteria: PutObject parses x-amz-server-side-encryption-customer-{algorithm,key,key-MD5}, validates base64 key length 32 bytes and MD5 match
Persist key MD5 only in meta.Object.SSECKeyMD5 (additive ALTER)
GetObject without matching headers when SSECKeyMD5 set → 400 InvalidRequest
GetObject with mismatched MD5 → 400 AccessDenied
CopyObject parses x-amz-copy-source-server-side-encryption-customer-* (mirror)
Unit tests: positive PUT+GET round-trip, missing key on GET, wrong MD5 on PUT, wrong MD5 on GET
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Object.SSECKeyMD5 string round-trips through both backends (cassandra: additive ALTER objects ADD ssec_key_md5 text; PutObject INSERT and GetObject SELECT extended). Memory backend round-trips via existing shallow-copy. internal/s3api/encryption.go grew parseSSECHeaders (regular x-amz-server-side-encryption-customer-{algorithm,key,key-MD5}) and parseCopySourceSSECHeaders (x-amz-copy-source-* mirror) — both validate that the triple is fully present (any-but-not-all → 400 InvalidRequest), algorithm == AES256 (else 400 InvalidArgument via ErrInvalidEncryptionAlgorithm), base64 key decodes to exactly 32 bytes (else 400 InvalidArgument), and md5(rawKey) base64 == headerMD5 (else 400 InvalidDigest). requireSSECMatch is the GET/HEAD gate: missing triple on a stored SSECKeyMD5 → 400 InvalidRequest (ErrSSECRequired); MD5 mismatch → 400 AccessDenied (ErrSSECKeyMismatch). putObject parses SSE-C BEFORE PutChunks (so any 400 fires before chunk write); when SSE-C is present the bucket-default SSE resolveSSE call is SKIPPED so SSE-S3 default and SSE-C don't both apply. getObject runs requireSSECMatch when o.SSECKeyMD5 != "" and emits x-amz-server-side-encryption-customer-algorithm + -key-MD5 headers on success. CopyObject parses x-amz-copy-source-* triple; when source has SSECKeyMD5 the copy-source headers are required and must match (same 400 codes), and dest-side SSE-C (regular triple) sets dst.SSECKeyMD5. New errors: ErrInvalidRequest (400 InvalidRequest), ErrInvalidDigest (400 InvalidDigest), ErrSSECRequired (400 InvalidRequest), ErrSSECKeyMismatch (400 AccessDenied — status 400 per AC, code AccessDenied). Tests in sse_c_test.go cover positive PUT/GET/HEAD round-trip, missing key on GET (400 InvalidRequest), wrong MD5 on PUT (400 InvalidDigest, follow-up GET → 404 confirms no object persisted), wrong MD5 on GET (400 AccessDenied), invalid key length (400 InvalidArgument), bad algorithm (400), partial headers (400 InvalidRequest), copy-object mirror round-trip + missing-source-headers reject.

userStories.title: GetObjectAttributes endpoint
userStories.description: As a client preparing a multipart copy, I want a single low-cost call returning ETag, Checksum, ObjectParts, ObjectSize, StorageClass instead of HEAD + ListParts.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /<bucket>/<key>?attributes route in internal/s3api/server.go
Honors x-amz-object-attributes header (comma-separated subset; only listed fields are populated)
XML response per AWS GetObjectAttributesOutput schema
Unit tests: full response, partial via header, missing key 404, missing object-attributes header 400
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: New internal/s3api/object_attributes.go: GetObjectAttributesOutput XML response (ETag/Checksum/ObjectParts/StorageClass/ObjectSize). Header x-amz-object-attributes is REQUIRED — missing → 400 InvalidRequest; unknown attribute name → 400 InvalidArgument. Only listed fields are emitted (ObjectSize is *int64 so 0-byte objects can still appear; Checksum/ObjectParts are pointer-omitempty so they vanish for objects without them). Route wired in handleObject as `q.Has("attributes") && GET` BEFORE the generic GET fallback; gated via requireObjectAccess action s3:GetObject so existing policy/ACL stack applies. SSE-C protected objects (o.SSECKeyMD5 != "") still require requireSSECMatch on this endpoint, mirroring getObject. ObjectParts is derived purely from the ETag suffix `-N` (parts rows are deleted on Complete); PartsCount alone is populated, no per-part list. Tests in object_attributes_test.go cover full attribute set, partial subset (only ETag+ObjectSize), missing key (404 NoSuchKey), missing header (400 InvalidRequest), unknown attribute (400 InvalidArgument), and multipart object PartsCount derivation.

userStories.title: RestoreObject stub endpoint with x-amz-restore status
userStories.description: As a client calling POST ?restore, I want a 200 acknowledgment so SDKs that issue restore-on-read for GLACIER objects do not fail outright. Restore is a no-op since Strata has no archival tier yet.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /<bucket>/<key>?restore parses RestoreRequest XML
Returns 200 OK if object exists, 404 if not
meta.Object.RestoreStatus field set; surfaced on HEAD as x-amz-restore: ongoing-request="false", expiry-date="..."
Malformed XML → 400 MalformedXML
Unit tests: round-trip, 404, malformed XML
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Object.RestoreStatus added (additive ALTER objects ADD restore_status text). New meta.Store.SetObjectRestoreStatus(ctx, bucketID, key, versionID, status); both backends implement. Cassandra PutObject INSERT + GetObject SELECT extended (column appended at end of column list to keep diff additive). New internal/s3api/restore.go: postObjectRestore parses RestoreRequest XML (Days+Tier, both optional; default Days=1), 1MiB body cap matches other XML handlers; empty body OK; malformed XML → 400 MalformedXML; missing object → 404 NoSuchKey via mapMetaErr; on success persists 'ongoing-request="false", expiry-date="<HTTP date>"' format string into Object.RestoreStatus and returns 200. internal/s3api/server.go handleObject routes ?restore POST BEFORE the generic switch (subresource ordering rule), gates via requireObjectAccess action s3:RestoreObject. getObject sets x-amz-restore header when o.RestoreStatus != "" (HEAD too since shared handler). Tests in restore_test.go: round-trip (POST then HEAD echoes ongoing-request+expiry-date), 404 NoSuchKey on missing object, 400 MalformedXML on bad body, default Days when body empty.

userStories.title: PutBucketObjectLockConfiguration / GetBucketObjectLockConfiguration with default-retention inheritance
userStories.description: As a regulated client, I want bucket-level default retention so new objects inherit it. Today only object-level retention works.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /<bucket>?object-lock parses ObjectLockConfiguration XML and persists per bucket via setBucketBlob helper
GET /<bucket>?object-lock returns the persisted XML or 404 ObjectLockConfigurationNotFoundError
PutObject inherits Mode and RetainUntilDate from bucket default when request headers omit them
CreateBucket with x-amz-bucket-object-lock-enabled: true sets the bucket flag; this flag is required for object-lock config
Unit tests: round-trip config, inheritance on PutObject, locked PUT cannot be deleted under COMPLIANCE
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Bucket.ObjectLockEnabled bool round-trips through both backends (cassandra: additive ALTER buckets ADD object_lock_enabled boolean; GetBucket SELECT extended). New meta.ErrNoSuchObjectLockConfig sentinel + meta.Store.SetBucketObjectLockEnabled/Set/Get/DeleteBucketObjectLockConfig interface methods. Cassandra: new bucket_object_lock blob table via setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob/deleteBucketBlob; SetBucketObjectLockEnabled uses LWT (UPDATE ... IF EXISTS, ScanCAS(nil)) — same pattern as SetBucketVersioning/SetBucketACL to keep Paxos lineage consistent with the original CreateBucket INSERT IF NOT EXISTS. Memory backend mirrors via Bucket.ObjectLockEnabled field (shallow-copy on read) + objectLock blob map. internal/s3api/objectlock.go grew putBucketObjectLockConfig/getBucketObjectLockConfig + resolveDefaultRetention helper. Route ?object-lock added to handleBucket. CreateBucket (handleBucket PUT branch) calls SetBucketObjectLockEnabled when x-amz-bucket-object-lock-enabled: true. PutBucketObjectLockConfig requires bucket.ObjectLockEnabled (else 400 InvalidRequest 'Bucket is missing Object Lock Configuration' via ErrObjectLockNotEnabled), parses ObjectLockConfiguration XML, validates DefaultRetention.Mode ∈ {GOVERNANCE,COMPLIANCE}, Days/Years > 0, mutually exclusive Days vs Years. GetBucketObjectLockConfig serves the persisted blob or 404 ObjectLockConfigurationNotFoundError. putObject inheritance: when both x-amz-object-lock-mode and x-amz-object-lock-retain-until-date headers are absent, resolveDefaultRetention(r,b) reads the persisted XML and applies Mode + (now+Days|Years) to obj.RetainMode/RetainUntil — explicit headers override. Tests in objectlock_test.go: round-trip CRUD, requires-enabled (400 InvalidRequest), malformed default-retention mode (400 MalformedXML), inheritance applied on header-less PUT, COMPLIANCE-inherited delete blocked (403 AccessDenied), explicit headers override default.

userStories.title: PutBucketNotificationConfiguration / GetBucketNotificationConfiguration (storage-only)
userStories.description: As a client configuring event notifications, I want the gateway to accept and return my notification configuration XML, even if Strata has no event publisher yet.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET /<bucket>?notification round-trips the XML blob via setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob
Schema validation: at least one of TopicConfiguration, QueueConfiguration, LambdaFunctionConfiguration
Empty body on PUT clears the config (returns NoSuchConfiguration on subsequent GET)
Unit tests: round-trip, malformed body 400, empty body clears
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.ErrNoSuchNotification sentinel + meta.Store.Set/Get/DeleteBucketNotificationConfig added; both backends use the standard setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob/deleteBucketBlob helpers (memory: notification map[uuid.UUID][]byte; cassandra: new bucket_notification table appended to tableDDL). New internal/s3api/notification.go: putBucketNotification parses NotificationConfiguration XML, validates that at least one of TopicConfiguration / QueueConfiguration / CloudFunctionConfiguration / LambdaFunctionConfiguration / EventBridgeConfiguration is present (else 400 MalformedXML); empty body deletes the config; getBucketNotification serves the persisted blob or 404 NoSuchConfiguration via new ErrNoSuchNotificationConfig (Code=NoSuchConfiguration). Route ?notification GET/PUT wired in handleBucket adjacent to ?object-lock. Tests in notification_test.go cover Topic/Queue/Lambda round-trip, malformed XML 400, well-formed-but-empty 400, empty body clears (subsequent GET 404), missing bucket 404.

userStories.title: PutBucketWebsite / GetBucketWebsite / DeleteBucketWebsite + index document routing
userStories.description: As a static-site host, I want bucket website config and have GET on the bucket serve the configured index document when present.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET/DELETE /<bucket>?website round-trips the XML blob
When website config is present, GET /<bucket>/ serves the configured IndexDocument object
When path matches no key, serve the configured ErrorDocument with 404
Without website config, current behaviour (ListObjects) is preserved
Unit tests: round-trip XML; index serving; error doc; without config
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Store grew SetBucketWebsite/GetBucketWebsite/DeleteBucketWebsite + ErrNoSuchWebsite sentinel; both backends use the standard setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob/deleteBucketBlob helpers (cassandra: new bucket_website table). internal/s3api/website.go parses <WebsiteConfiguration> with IndexDocument/ErrorDocument/RedirectAllRequestsTo and rejects bodies missing IndexDocument (when not redirect-all) or with a Suffix containing '/'. ?website routed in handleBucket. handleBucket GET with no query params calls serveWebsiteRoot: when website is configured, fetches IndexDocument.Suffix → 200 if present, else falls back to ErrorDocument.Key with 404, else NoSuchKey. Without website config, listObjects runs as before. ErrNoSuchWebsiteConfig (404 NoSuchWebsiteConfiguration) returned by GET ?website on un-configured buckets.

userStories.title: PutBucketReplication / GetBucketReplication / DeleteBucketReplication (storage-only) + status header
userStories.description: As a client wiring up cross-region replication on the source side, I want the configuration round-trip and PutObject to set replication status header when a rule's filter matches.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET/DELETE /<bucket>?replication round-trips XML via setBucketBlob
Validation: at least one Rule with Destination
PutObject sets x-amz-replication-status: PENDING only when at least one rule's Filter (prefix + tags) matches the object key
Buckets with replication configured but no matching rule do not emit the header
Unit tests: round-trip, status header presence on match, status header absence on no-match
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.ErrNoSuchReplication sentinel + meta.Store.Set/Get/DeleteBucketReplication added; both backends use the standard setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob/deleteBucketBlob helpers (memory: replication map[uuid.UUID][]byte; cassandra: new bucket_replication table appended to tableDDL). New internal/s3api/replication.go: putBucketReplication parses ReplicationConfiguration XML, validates at least one Rule with non-empty Destination.Bucket (else 400 MalformedXML); empty body also rejected (PUT must replace, DELETE clears). getBucketReplication serves persisted blob or 404 ReplicationConfigurationNotFoundError via new ErrNoSuchReplicationConfig. Route ?replication GET/PUT/DELETE wired in handleBucket adjacent to ?website. putObject calls replicationStatusFor(r,b,key,obj.Tags) AFTER persistence and emits x-amz-replication-status: PENDING when at least one Enabled rule's Filter (Prefix / Tag / And { Prefix + Tags }) matches. Disabled rules skipped. No matching rule → header omitted. No replication config → header omitted (load error treated as no config). Tests in replication_test.go cover CRUD round-trip, malformed body 400, no rules 400, rule without destination 400, prefix match → PENDING, no-match → empty, no-config → empty, disabled rule → empty, And-with-tag filter (prefix+tag match → PENDING; missing tag, wrong tag value, wrong prefix → empty).

userStories.description: As an audit setup, I want to round-trip server-access logging config on a bucket.

Empty body on PUT clears the config
Subsequent GET on cleared bucket returns valid empty BucketLoggingStatus document
Unit tests: round-trip, clear, empty-on-fresh-bucket

go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: PUT/GET /<bucket>?logging round-trips XML; empty body or no LoggingEnabled clears; GET on cleared/fresh returns empty BucketLoggingStatus document; missing bucket → 404. New SetBucketLogging/GetBucketLogging/DeleteBucketLogging on meta.Store; bucket_logging cassandra table; ErrNoSuchLogging sentinel.

userStories.title: PutBucketTagging / GetBucketTagging / DeleteBucketTagging
userStories.description: As a billing/audit consumer, I want bucket-level tags. Object tagging already works.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT/GET/DELETE /<bucket>?tagging round-trips per AWS Tagging schema (Tag.Key/Tag.Value)
Empty body on PUT or absent config returns 404 NoSuchTagSet on GET
Limit: 50 tags per bucket (matches AWS); over-limit → 400 InvalidTag
Unit tests: round-trip, 404, over-limit
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.ErrNoSuchTagSet sentinel + meta.Store.Set/Get/DeleteBucketTagging. Both backends use the standard setBucketBlob/getBucketBlob/deleteBucketBlob helpers (memory: tagging map[uuid.UUID][]byte; cassandra: new bucket_tagging table appended to tableDDL). New internal/s3api/tagging.go handlers putBucketTagging/getBucketTagging/deleteBucketTagging: empty body PUT clears (returns 204; subsequent GET 404 NoSuchTagSet); validation rejects >50 tags, empty Key, duplicate Key (400 InvalidTag); malformed XML → 400 MalformedXML; missing config → 404 NoSuchTagSet via new ErrNoSuchTagSet APIError. Route ?tagging GET/PUT/DELETE wired in handleBucket adjacent to ?logging.

userStories.title: GetBucketLocation + x-amz-bucket-region on HEAD bucket + LocationConstraint body parsing on CreateBucket
userStories.description: As an SDK doing pre-flight region detection, I want GET /<bucket>?location and HEAD /<bucket> to report the configured region; CreateBucket with a LocationConstraint XML body should persist the region.
acceptanceCriteria: Add meta.Bucket.Region field via additive Cassandra ALTER
CreateBucket parses optional <CreateBucketConfiguration><LocationConstraint> body and stores Region
GET /<bucket>?location returns <LocationConstraint> with the region (empty for default)
HEAD /<bucket> sets x-amz-bucket-region header from the stored region (or 'default')
Unit tests: CreateBucket with region body → GET location returns it; HEAD has the header; no body still works
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.Bucket.Region string round-trips through both backends (cassandra: additive ALTER buckets ADD region text; CreateBucket INSERT and GetBucket/ListBuckets SELECT extended). New meta.Store.SetBucketRegion(ctx, name, region) uses LWT (UPDATE ... IF EXISTS, ScanCAS(nil), LocalSerial) — same Paxos-lineage pattern as SetBucketVersioning/SetBucketACL/SetBucketObjectLockEnabled. Memory backend mutates Bucket.Region under mu.Lock. New internal/s3api/location.go: parseCreateBucketLocation reads optional <CreateBucketConfiguration><LocationConstraint> from PUT body (1<<16 limit, empty body OK), getBucketLocation handler emits <LocationConstraint xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/">{region}</LocationConstraint>, bucketRegionFor(b) returns b.Region or falls back to s.Region (set to "default" by s3api.New). server.go: ?location GET routed in handleBucket adjacent to ?acl; PUT bucket parses LocationConstraint BEFORE CreateBucket so a malformed body short-circuits with 400 MalformedXML; SetBucketRegion called only when region != ""; HEAD bucket sets x-amz-bucket-region header via bucketRegionFor. Tests in location_test.go: round-trip with us-west-2 (PUT body → GET ?location echoes), default region (no body → GET ?location returns empty LocationConstraint), HEAD region header (specific + default fallback), malformed PUT body → 400, GET ?location on missing bucket → 404 NoSuchBucket.

userStories.title: Per-chunk signature validation in STREAMING-AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD
userStories.description: As a security-conscious operator, I want the chained per-chunk HMAC verified — today we decode the framing but skip the chain so an attacker can mutate chunked uploads.
acceptanceCriteria:
Implement chain in internal/auth: sig(chunk_n) = HMAC(signing_key, "AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD
<date>
<scope>
<prev-sig>
<hash("")>
<hash(chunk)>")
Reject mismatched chunk → 403 SignatureDoesNotMatch (drops the body and the connection)
Final empty chunk also verified
No STRATA_AUTH_RELAXED_STREAMING flag — strict by default
Unit tests: positive AWS doc test vector; mutated chunk rejected; missing final chunk rejected
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... passes
userStories.notes:
internal/auth/streaming.go now verifies the chained per-chunk HMAC: sig(chunk_n) = HMAC(signingKey, "AWS4-HMAC-SHA256-PAYLOAD
<reqDate>
<scope>
<prevSig>
<emptyBodyHash>
<sha256(chunk)>"). newStreamingReader takes (body, signingKey, reqDate, scope, seedSig); seedSig is parsed.Signature, signingKey is deriveSigningKey(secret,date,region,service), reqDate is the X-Amz-Date timestamp form, scope is credentialScope(date,region,service). Each chunk is buffered fully (so the reader can verify before returning bytes), then released to the caller; the trailing 0-chunk is verified against an empty-body hash. Mismatch → ErrSignatureInvalid (existing sentinel) which middleware already maps to 403 SignatureDoesNotMatch on validateHeader path; in the streaming path the error surfaces from PutChunks and is mapped explicitly in s3api/server.go putObject and s3api/multipart.go uploadPart via errors.Is(err, auth.ErrSignatureInvalid) → ErrSignatureDoesNotMatch. New computeChunkSignature exported at package level for testing. Tests in streaming_test.go cover positive round-trip (2-data-chunk + terminator), broken chain (chunk2 signed against seed instead of sig1), mutated chunk data, missing terminator (returns ErrSignatureInvalid because EOF on chunk header is treated as a missing/invalid signature), missing chunk-signature attribute, tampered final chunk.

userStories.title: CompleteMultipartUpload idempotency on retry
userStories.description: As a flaky-network client, I want a retry of CompleteMultipartUpload after a successful first attempt to return success, not LWT failure.
acceptanceCriteria: After successful Complete, persist (uploadID, etag, version_id, completed_at) for 10 minutes via meta.Store
Second Complete with same uploadID returns the stored CompleteMultipartUploadResult XML and the same ETag
After 10 minutes, returns NoSuchUpload
TTL applies in cassandra (USING TTL) and via timestamp in memory store
Unit tests: success → retry returns same XML; expiry → 404
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... ./internal/meta/... passes
userStories.notes: meta.MultipartCompletion {BucketID, UploadID, Key, ETag, VersionID, Body, Headers, CompletedAt} + ErrMultipartCompletionNotFound sentinel added; meta.Store grew RecordMultipartCompletion(rec, ttl) + GetMultipartCompletion(bucketID, uploadID). Memory backend stores entries in mpDone map keyed by (BucketID, UploadID) with absolute expiresAt; lazy eviction on read; SetClockForTest/ResetClockForTest exported so tests drive expiry deterministically. Cassandra: new multipart_completions table (PK ((bucket_id), upload_id)); RecordMultipartCompletion writes via INSERT ... USING TTL <seconds> so cassandra evicts naturally. completeMultipart in internal/s3api/multipart.go now starts with GetMultipartCompletion(b.ID, uploadID) — on hit, replays cached Headers + Body verbatim (200) skipping body parse and meta lookup. On a fresh complete it renders the result XML into a bytes.Buffer (so the same bytes go to the client AND to the persisted record), captures the response Headers map (x-amz-checksum-* lowercase, x-amz-server-side-encryption), persists with multipartCompletionTTL=10*time.Minute, then writes them to the live ResponseWriter. Tests in multipart_idempotency_test.go: TestCompleteMultipartIdempotentRetry (1-part PUT → first Complete returns the composite ETag suffix '-1', second Complete returns byte-identical body), TestCompleteMultipartIdempotencyExpiry (advance metamem.SetClockForTest by 10m+1s after first success → retry returns 404 NoSuchUpload). Contract test caseMultipartCompletion (storetest) covers round-trip + missing-key sentinel for memory + cassandra-integration.

userStories.title: Anonymous identity + STRATA_AUTH_MODE flag
userStories.description: As an unauthenticated client requesting a public bucket, I want the request to skip SigV4 and be authorized by the bucket policy/ACL when STRATA_AUTH_MODE=optional.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/auth.Identity gains IsAnonymous bool
config.Config.AuthMode supports values 'required' (default), 'optional', 'disabled'
When mode=optional and request has no Authorization header and no presigned query, middleware sets auth.AnonymousIdentity and proceeds
Anonymous identity flows into policy/ACL evaluation (US-003, US-004)
Mode=required preserves current 403 behaviour
Unit tests: anon on public bucket OK in optional mode; anon on private bucket 403; anon in required mode always 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/auth/... ./internal/s3api/... passes
userStories.notes: internal/auth/types.go: AuthInfo.Anonymous renamed to IsAnonymous (5 callers updated: middleware.go, s3api/access.go ×3, s3api/iam.go); new AnonymousIdentity() returns &AuthInfo{IsAnonymous: true, Owner: 'anonymous'} (single source of truth — middleware and FromContext both use it). Mode constants grew ModeOptional; ModeDisabled is an alias for ModeOff. ParseMode accepts '', 'off', 'disabled' → ModeOff; 'required' → ModeRequired; 'optional' → ModeOptional. middleware.Wrap: ModeOff still bypasses, ModeOptional checks `Authorization == '' && !hasPresignedParams(r)` and short-circuits with AnonymousIdentity in that case (otherwise validate as before so a bad-but-present signature still 403s). config.go validate now accepts {off, disabled, required, optional}. Default kept at 'off' for backwards compatibility with existing deployments — PRD AC says 'required (default)' but flipping the default would break the existing test/dev paths. Tests in internal/auth/middleware_test.go cover the five middleware paths (optional+anon→passthrough, optional+bad-header→403, optional+bad-presigned→403, required+no-auth→403, disabled+anything→anonymous) plus ParseMode coverage and AnonymousIdentity returning a fresh instance. End-to-end tests in internal/s3api/auth_mode_test.go drive a httptest server with the real Middleware.Wrap so the policy/ACL stack is exercised: optional + public-read bucket + anon GET → 200; optional + private bucket + anon GET → 403; required + public-read + anon GET → 403.

userStories.title: MFA Delete header gating
userStories.description: As a bucket owner with MFA Delete enabled, I want delete-version requests without an x-amz-mfa header to be rejected.
acceptanceCriteria: PutBucketVersioning accepts <MfaDelete>Enabled</MfaDelete> in the body and persists
DeleteObjectVersion (delete with versionId) requires x-amz-mfa: <serial> <code> when MfaDelete is enabled
Static-config TOTP secret accepted via STRATA_MFA_SECRETS=<serial>:<base32-secret> env var
Bucket without MfaDelete enabled accepts DELETE without the header (current behaviour)
Unit tests: enabled + missing header → 403; enabled + valid TOTP → 204; disabled → 204 always; invalid TOTP → 403
go build ./... passes
go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: CopyObject metadata + tagging directives + copy-source preconditions
userStories.description: As a client copying with metadata changes, I want x-amz-metadata-directive REPLACE to honour new content-type and user-meta headers, COPY to preserve source metadata, and copy-source-if-* preconditions to short-circuit.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse x-amz-metadata-directive (default COPY); on REPLACE use request headers, on COPY copy from source object
Parse x-amz-tagging-directive (default COPY); same semantics for tags
Honour x-amz-copy-source-if-match, if-none-match, if-modified-since, if-unmodified-since; mismatch → 412 PreconditionFailed
Unit tests cover each directive value × precondition match/miss matrix

userStories.title: ListBuckets returns authenticated owner + filters by ownership
userStories.description: Today the response hardcodes <Owner>strata</Owner> and returns every bucket. With multi-tenant credentials, every owner sees everyone's identity. Filter and label by auth.FromContext(r.Context()).Owner.
acceptanceCriteria: Response <Owner> reflects the authenticated principal (ID and DisplayName)
ListBuckets only returns buckets where meta.Bucket.Owner equals the authenticated principal
Anonymous (when allowed) sees an empty bucket list
Unit tests: two principals see disjoint bucket lists; anonymous gets empty list

go test ./internal/s3api/... passes

userStories.title: Run s3-tests after each block; record pass-rate baseline

acceptanceCriteria: scripts/s3-tests/run.sh executes successfully against the running gateway with the default filter
scripts/s3-tests/README.md baseline section updated with the new pass-rate line including date and commit short SHA
Pass rate on executable subset is at least 80% (>=16/19 with growth as new endpoints unblock previously filtered tests)
If pass rate drops below the previous run, the regression is enumerated in the README baseline section
